topic.title: SAP EWM Integration with SAP TM

learningObjectives.title: Understand TM-EWM integration
learningObjectives.description: Explain synchronization between transportation planning and warehouse execution.

learningObjectives.title: Understand exchanged objects
learningObjectives.description: Describe business objects and status updates shared across SAP TM and SAP EWM.

learningObjectives.title: Understand execution coordination
learningObjectives.description: Clarify warehouse readiness and loading confirmations before shipment execution.

sections.content: SAP Extended Warehouse Management (SAP EWM) integrates closely with SAP Transportation Management to synchronize warehouse operations with transportation activities. While SAP TM plans and manages transportation, SAP EWM executes warehouse processes such as picking, packing, staging, loading and unloading. Together they provide end-to-end logistics execution.

sections.title: Key Concept
sections.content: SAP TM decides WHEN and HOW goods move. SAP EWM manages HOW goods are physically handled inside the warehouse.

sections.title: SAP TM & SAP EWM Integration Data

sections.content: A Freight Order is created in SAP TM for delivery from a distribution center. SAP EWM receives the transportation request, generates warehouse tasks, completes picking and packing, schedules dock loading and confirms loading completion. SAP TM then updates the Freight Order and releases the shipment for transportation.

sections.title: Best Practice
sections.content: Synchronize warehouse confirmations with transportation execution so Freight Orders are released only after loading activities are completed successfully.

sections.title: Real Project Example
sections.content: A consumer goods manufacturer integrated SAP TM with SAP EWM across 12 warehouses. Automatic warehouse confirmations reduced truck waiting time by 35% and improved on-time departures.

sections.title: Interview Tip
sections.content: Remember the division of responsibilities: SAP TM manages transportation planning and execution, while SAP EWM manages warehouse execution. They complement each other rather than replacing each other.

sections.title: Common Mistake
sections.content: SAP TM does not perform warehouse picking or packing. Those activities are executed in SAP EWM and synchronized back to SAP TM through integration.

items: SAP EWM manages warehouse execution activities.
SAP TM manages transportation planning and execution.
Both systems exchange Freight Orders, loading status and execution events.
Integration improves loading efficiency and shipment visibility.
Warehouse completion triggers transportation execution in SAP TM.
